title: Lessons Learned in Teaching Electrochemistry
authors: Alanah Fitch, Ingrid Fritsch, Carol Korzeniewski, Shelley D. Minteer and Johna Leddy
abstract: Students frequently encounter difficulties in learning the vast landscape of electrochemistry. Here are shared examples of methods to demystify electrochemistry based on the extensive experience of the authors. Topics include a perspective on the shape of a voltammogram viewed as Ohm’s Law, an extrapolation to <mark>electrochemical impedance</mark> spectroscopy (EIS), potential axes to identify spontaneous reactions, and select examples from organic and biological electrochemistry. Explained is the rationale behind certain Go To molecules used in electrochemical experiments such as ferrocene, ferricyanide,…

title: Design Trade-Offs in Composite Fuel Cell Membranes: Effects of Reinforcement and Chemical Additives
authors: Douglas I. Kushner, Grace Lau, Athena Y. Liu and Ahmet Kusoglu
abstract: Perfluorosulfonic acid (PFSA) membranes are critical components in proton exchange membrane fuel cells, where performance depends on balancing <mark>ionic conductivity</mark>, mechanical durability, and chemical stability. This study characterizes a composite membrane (NC700) featuring PFSA-impregnated expanded polytetrafluoroethylene (ePTFE) reinforcement and cerium-based radical scavengers, benchmarked against unreinforced NR211. Complementary techniques, including electron microscopy, X-ray scattering, infrared spectroscopy, thermogravimetric analysis, and dynamic mechanical analysis, identify the struc…

title: In-Situ Hydrothermal Preparation of Sandwich Structural Nickel-Based Electrodes for Efficient Oxygen Evolution Reaction in Alkaline Electrolyte
authors: Jiaxi Peng, Mengxin Yu, Linzi Zhang, Xiaoxin Wu and Wenyuan Xu
abstract: Oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is important for water splitting and CO2 electrochemical reduction. However, the current electrodes for OER still suffer from serious issues such as high potentials and poor stability due to the low electrical conductivity, poor electron transfer, etc. Herein, hierarchical sandwich structural Ni-based electrodes without any polymer binders to increase transportation resistance and block active sites are directly arrayed on carbon cloth (CC) by an in situ hydrothermal reaction. The OER activities of different sequentially arranged Ni-based electrodes are investig…
